title: Issue Resolution Time Tracking
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:issue`. from the `issue` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per active issue per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `priority` (string), `category` (string), `name` (string), `deviceName` (string), `status` (string), `issueId` (string), `siteId` (string), `lastOccurenceTime` (epoch milliseconds).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" status="RESOLVED" | eval resolve_time_hrs=round((resolved_time-issue_time)/3600, 1) | stats avg(resolve_time_hrs) as avg_resolve_hrs median(resolve_time_hrs) as median_resolve_hrs max(resolve_time_hrs) as max_resolve_hrs by priority, category | sort priority
description: Measures how long it takes to resolve Catalyst Center assurance issues by priority and category, tracking mean time to resolve (MTTR).
value: MTTR is a key operational metric. Tracking resolution times by priority and category reveals bottlenecks and measures the effectiveness of the operations team.
implementation: Enable the `issue` input. Ensure `issue_time` and `resolved_time` (epoch seconds) are present in events or normalized via the TA. Validate field names if your build uses different aliases.
visualization: Table (avg, median, max resolve hours by priority and category), bar chart of median MTTR by category.

references.title: Catalyst Center Issues API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Issues auto-resolved by Assurance showing artificially short resolution times.** Some issues are raised and resolved by the Assurance engine within minutes without human intervention (e.g., brief connectivity flaps). These skew average resolution times downward. Distinguish by checking whether `resolve_time_hrs < 0.1` (under 6 minutes), indicating auto-resolution. Suppress by filtering `| where resolve_time_hrs >= 0.5` for meaningful MTTR tracking.

**Issue resolution timestamp not populated for manually closed issues.** If an operator marks an issue as RESOLVED in Catalyst Center without a proper timestamp, the resolution time calculation may be incorrect or null. Distinguish by checking whether `resolved_time` is populated. Suppress by filtering `| where isnotnull(resolved_time) AND resolved_time>issue_time` before computing MTTR.

**Maintenance window issues with known resolution start time.** Issues created during maintenance windows are expected to be resolved once the maintenance completes. Their resolution time reflects the maintenance duration, not the team's operational response capability. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM maintenance records. Suppress by excluding maintenance-window issues from MTTR calculations using a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup.

**Issue re-opened after initial resolution resetting the resolution timer.** Some issues may recur after initial resolution, effectively restarting the resolution clock. Distinguish by checking whether the same `issueId` appears multiple times with different `status` values. Suppress by tracking by `issueId` and using the final resolution time for MTTR calculation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **issue** modular input to `cisco:dnac:issue` in `index=catalyst` (Cisco Catalyst Add-on 7538).
• **Validate field names** for **open** and **close** times on a **RESOLVED** raw event: `issue_time` and `resolved_time` are assumed to be **epoch seconds** as the SPL expects. If your TA uses **milliseconds** or **ISO strings**, add a normalizing **eval** in a macro before this search.
• **Status token** for closed issues (commonly **`RESOLVED`**) must match **case** your JSON uses—adjust the search filter if your tenant differs.
• `docs/implementation-guide.md` for retention (MTTR reports often need **90–180 days** of issues).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues` (paged issue list).
• **TA input:** **issue**; typical **300–900s** poll; confirm whether **closed** issues are re-sent each cycle (affects how many **RESOLVED** rows you have per `issueId`).
• **Deduplication:** for MTTR, prefer **one row per `issueId` per state transition**; if the TA **replays** the same resolved issue, use **`| stats latest(resolved_time) as resolved_time ... by issueId`** in an intermediate saved search or **dedup** in validation first.

Step 2 — Create the report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" status="RESOLVED" | eval resolve_time_hrs=round((resolved_time-issue_time)/3600, 1) | stats avg(resolve_time_hrs) as avg_resolve_hrs median(resolve_time_hrs) as median_resolve_hrs max(resolve_time_hrs) as max_resolve_hrs by priority, category | sort priority
```

Understanding this SPL (time math, outliers, P1 first)
• **Negative** `resolve_time_hrs` means **data error** (clock skew or swapped fields); add **`| where resolve_time_hrs>=0`** in production.
• **Max** is sensitive to a single long-running TAC case; show **p95** in a v2 for leadership slides.
• **`sort priority`** is **lexicographic**—document if **P10**-style values ever appear.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
• Keeps **resolved** work only, converts duration to **hours**, aggregates **mean/median/max** by **priority** and **Assurance** **category**.

Step 3 — Validate
• Hand-check **2–3** issues in the **Catalyst** UI: compare **created/resolved** times to `issue_time`/`resolved_time` in Splunk in the same **TZ**.
• **`| timechart` count** of **RESOLVED** to ensure steady ingest, not a **one-time** bulk import that distorts **avg** for the week.
• **Sanity cap:** if **median** is **>10 days** and that surprises the team, segment by **category** and exclude **'informational'** in a v2.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** table of **avg/median/max**; **add** a **bar** of **median** by **category** for QBRs.
• **Cadence:** **monthly** PDF for service owners; not typically a **real-time** alert—pair with **UC-5.13.25** (repeaters) to drive **root cause** work.

• **Validate:** compare against the Catalyst Center Assurance Issues dashboard — verify P1/P2 counts match for the same time window.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **NULL times:** `issue_time` / `resolved_time` not extracted—check **JSON** path in **props** and TA version.
• **Zeros everywhere:** you may have **ingest** of **placeholders**; confirm **raw** has non-zero epoch values.
• **Dramatic change after upgrade:** field rename in **Assurance** API—`fieldsummary` the **release week** and update **aliases** in **props**.
• **No issue events arriving:** verify the `issue` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing fields or empty values:** some issue fields vary by Catalyst Center version; run `| fieldsummary` to identify NULL or zero values that need `fillnull`.
• **Fewer events than the Catalyst Center UI shows:** the API returns only active issues by default; if credential access is denied, check the service account RBAC role.
grandmaExplanation: We look at how long it took to close each kind of problem Catalyst Center reported, in plain hours, so we can see where fixes drag on and where the team is quick.
